title: arenaria aggregata subsp. tomentosa
intro: Arenaria aggregata subsp. tomentosa, also known as woolly rockjasmine, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues. The health benefits of woolly rockjasmine include reducing fever and relieving respiratory and digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of woolly rockjasmine can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
